concerns.text: Task T016 explicitly states: 'overriding FR-008's reaction class stratification'. This is a direct semantic weakening of FR-008 which mandates stratification by reaction class. A task cannot unilaterally override a Functional Requirement; the spec must be updated to reflect the Scaffold Split methodology before the task is valid.

Task T037 ('Update plan.md to reflect the Scaffold Split implementation and resolve FR-008 inconsistency') treats the spec/plan contradiction as a post-implementation cleanup. The plan already contradicts the spec (Plan: 'Critical Methodology Update... contradicts FR-008'). This inconsistency must be resolved in the spec/plan artifacts BEFORE the tasks are generated to ensure the task list does not encode a violation of the current spec.

Tasks T026 and T027 implement a complex statistical significance test (p-value, 95% CI) and a three-tier flagging system. Spec FR-005 and US2 Acceptance Scenario 3 mandate a binary flag ('Practically Significant' if >= 0.10, else 'No Significant Improvement'). The tasks implement a scope expansion (statistical rigor) not authorized by the current spec, effectively weakening the strict binary requirement defined in FR-005.
